hard.next_step: May 2026: Met team at Gartner Conference and interested in a follow up Match 2026: unfortunately haven’t received any feedback yet. It seems the RFI has not gained much traction so far, despite my offer to organise a design workshop to help define our next steps. I’ll continue to nudge them and keep you posted if anything changes. Oct 10, 2025 Following up with this prospect as they are in their planning stage for a project We are planning to attack through EY and we'll invite Sasol to the roundtable event.
hard.ais_why:
• 0 calls found in Avoma over last 12 months — no transcript data available.
• Cannot assess AI interest without conversational evidence.
• Recommend scheduling discovery call to probe AI/automation interest.

north_star_verdict.headline: Dark since 15 Jan 2026 (about 172 days with no buyer touch): the RFI stalled, the offered design workshop was never taken up, and the EY partner-led motion has produced no logged progress.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2027-02-26 is about 235 days out from today (2026-07-06). Created 2025-07-14, so this opportunity is roughly 358 days old and only reached Qualified on 2026-07-01 after bouncing Qualified to Initial Interest (30 Mar 2026) and back. Last verified buyer activity was 2026-01-15, about 172 days ago, which forces a dark read. The seven 2026-07-01 Call-Attempted tasks are rep-side health nudges, not buyer engagement. Pace to close cannot be assessed while the buyer is unresponsive; re-engagement, not pace, is the gating issue.
evidence: LastActivityDate 2026-01-15, about 172 days ago, forces a dark state (SF LastActivityDate)
Next_Step__c March 2026: RFI has not gained much traction, design-workshop offer not accepted (SF Next_Step__c)
Next_Step__c May 2026: met team at Gartner Conference, interested in follow-up, but no follow-up activity logged since (SF Next_Step__c)
Field history 2026-07-01: StageName Initial Interest to Qualified by Caroline Lacocque, a rep-side re-qualification not a buyer commitment (SF OpportunityFieldHistory)
No Avoma calls discovered for this opportunity or account (AIS_Why__c: 0 calls in 12 months)
No OpportunityContactRole designated Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or Champion (SF OpportunityContactRole)

metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, savings target, or cost-of-inaction has been captured for Sasol. There are no Avoma calls to extract buyer-stated pain, and the synthesis-map metric fields returned no data. The only signal is the October 2025 Next_Step note that Sasol is in the planning stage for a project, which implies a future-state initiative rather than an acute, quantified pain. The product scope (ML iAnalyze, iSave, iSupplier, iRisk) points at spend visibility, savings identification, supplier and risk management, but no Sasol stakeholder has confirmed these are priorities or attached a number to them. The move is a discovery or re-engagement call to surface and quantify the operational or financial problem, the cost of delay, and the expected ROI.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is identified or engaged. None of the five OpportunityContactRole contacts carries an Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor designation, and the synthesis-map budget fields returned no data. The most senior mapped contact is Hendrik Steynberg, VP Inbound Supply Chain Management, a plausible operational sponsor but not a confirmed budget owner, and he has not been engaged since January 2026. With no call history there is no evidence of who controls commercial or pricing authority at Sasol. Identifying and reaching the budget owner (likely at supply-chain or finance VP level, above the operational leads) is a ranked move.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (5 contacts, none EB/Exec Sponsor)

decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria Sasol would score vendors against are unknown. The March 2026 Next_Step references an RFI, which normally encodes formal requirements, but the RFI content was never captured and the note says it gained no traction. No use-case reviews, test cases, or workshop feedback exist because no calls were held. Without the RFI requirements or any discovery we cannot say what Sasol values (spend analytics depth, supplier risk, AI/automation, ERP fit). The move is to obtain the RFI or run a requirements-discovery session so the criteria can be documented and mapped to Zycus differentiators.

decision_process.narrative: The buying and approval process Sasol is running is not documented. The stage history shows rep-driven churn (Qualified to the relevant stakeholder on 30 Mar 2026 and back to Qualified on 01 Jul 2026) rather than observed buyer milestones. An RFI was referenced in March 2026 but no procurement owner, evaluation timeline, or approval chain has been confirmed, and there are no calls to read the process from a meeting sequence. As a large South African chemicals and energy enterprise, Sasol will almost certainly run a formal, multi-stakeholder procurement, but the specifics are unmapped. The move is to ask a mapped contact to walk through the evaluation stages and approval steps.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec approval, and implementation scoping are all unknown. No MSA, SOW, or NDA activity is logged and no calls exist to reveal the paper path. Given Sasol's size and South African base, cross-border data residency, POPIA compliance, and likely SAP ERP integration are probable contract and implementation topics, but none have been scoped. The October 2025 Next_Step mentioned attacking through EY and inviting Sasol to a roundtable, yet no EY engagement or scoping has been logged. The move is to qualify the procurement and legal path and confirm whether EY has any role, once a live buyer conversation is re-established.

identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain Sasol is trying to solve has not been articulated. The only signal is the October 2025 planning-stage reference, which describes intent rather than an acute problem, and there is no call evidence of the buyer describing manual-process burden, savings leakage, supplier risk, or compliance exposure. The product scope suggests those pain areas but the buyer has not confirmed them and no pain owner has been named. The move is a structured pain-discovery session with the operational leads (Hendrik Steynberg, Deon Meyer) to surface the problem, name the pain owner, and quantify the cost of inaction.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified or developed. Five operational contacts are on the opportunity (Hendrik Steynberg, VP Inbound Supply Chain Management; Deon Meyer, Manager: the deal owner's manager / Project Portfolio Manager; Jacobus de Wet, Lead Sourcing: Facilities and Civil (Acting); Estee Larkins, Snr Commercial Contract Specialist at Sasol; Thabiso Seboka, Improvement Specialist), but none is designated Champion and none has shown advocacy, introduced Zycus to power, or driven internal momentum. The March 2026 Next_Step states the RFI stalled despite the offered design workshop, a strong signal that no internal sponsor is pushing on Zycus's behalf, and the 172-day silence reinforces it. The move is to re-engage Hendrik Steynberg or Deon Meyer, test their willingness to advocate, and if neither will, recruit a different internal sponsor.
sources: SF Next_Step__c (March 2026 workshop offer not taken up)

competition.narrative: The competitive landscape is unknown, not sole-sourced by choice. Competitors__c is empty, the free-text competition fields returned no data, and there are no calls to reveal rivals or the incumbent stack. The March 2026 RFI reference implies a possible multi-vendor evaluation, but no vendor is named and the initiative may equally be early and running against the status quo. For a large procurement transformation the real competition is most likely Sasol's current manual or ERP-native procurement reporting plus internal inertia, which the RFI stall and 172-day silence are consistent with. The move is a direct discovery call to map the competitive set, understand the current procurement and analytics stack, and confirm whether the RFI is multi-vendor.

deal_movement.summary: Movement over the last year is rep-side administration, not buyer progress: stage churned Qualified to Initial Interest (30 Mar 2026) and back to Qualified (01 Jul 2026), the close date slipped twice (2026-03-31 to 2026-06-30 to 2027-02-26), and ownership changed hands. Last genuine buyer touch was 2026-01-15; everything since is Call-Attempted health nudges and a Gartner conference contact that produced no follow-up.

competitive_position.summary: No named vendor rival can be asserted on this evidence: Competitors__c is empty, no Avoma calls exist to name rivals or the incumbent, and the March 2026 RFI reference names no vendor. The single strongest CURRENT threat is do-nothing / status quo, consistent with the stalled RFI, the declined design-workshop offer, and 172 days of silence: Sasol keeps managing procurement analytics with its current manual or ERP-native reporting and never advances a purchase. Mapping the real competitive set is a discovery task, not a settled read.

best_practice_check.summary: This is a dark, un-discovered early-stage deal: the priority is to restart a real buyer conversation and run first-time discovery, not to chase a stale RFI or push a workshop the buyer already declined.
flags: Deal is dark 172 days: the owner must personally re-open with Hendrik Steynberg before any escalation or workshop re-pitch.
Zero discovery on a $200k enterprise deal: run a first pain-and-criteria call to replace assumptions about ML iAnalyze / iSave / iSupplier / iRisk fit.
Single un-mapped power base: no Economic Buyer or champion among the five operational contacts, so identify the budget owner above VP Inbound Supply Chain.

champion_strength.summary: No champion has been identified or developed. Five operational contacts are on the opportunity but none is designated Champion, none has shown advocacy, and the March 2026 declined design-workshop offer plus the 172-day silence indicate no internal sponsor is driving Zycus. Recruiting a champion is a prerequisite to any commercial progress.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The Merlin / agentic AI story has never been put in front of Sasol: AIS is 0 / Negative purely because no calls exist to assess AI appetite, not because the buyer rejected AI. Positioning is untested, so the agentic narrative is a discovery opportunity once a live conversation resumes.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score__c 0.0 / AIS_Status__c Negative, driven entirely by 0 Avoma calls in 12 months, so there is no conversational evidence of AI interest either way. Treat this as unmeasured, not resistant: the ML iAnalyze scope hints at automation appetite that must be probed on a first call.

items.detail: No Economic Buyer or Executive Sponsor mapped among the five operational contacts; no access to budget-owning power above VP Inbound Supply Chain.
No champion driving Zycus internally; the declined design workshop shows no internal sponsor pushing the initiative forward.
Do-nothing / status-quo inertia is the primary threat: the RFI stalled and the buyer has gone quiet for 172 days, a no-decision risk rather than a loss to a rival.
Close date slipped twice (2026-03-31 to 2026-06-30 to 2027-02-26) with no buyer-confirmed timeline; the current date is a placeholder, not a buyer commitment.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Sasol is a dark, un-discovered early-stage deal: no buyer conversation has ever been recorded, the RFI stalled, and the account has been silent for 172 days, so the only path forward is to re-open a real dialogue and run first-time discovery.

win_position.text: We are not positioned to win yet: no Economic Buyer, champion, pain, or competitive read exists across any source, and the buyer has been silent since 15 Jan 2026 (SF LastActivityDate + OpportunityContactRole).
The RFI stalled and Caroline Lacocque's design-workshop offer was never taken up, showing no internal sponsor is driving Zycus (Next_Step__c, March 2026).
AIS is 0 / Negative only because 0 Avoma calls exist in 12 months, so our AI and product fit for ML iAnalyze / iSave / iSupplier / iRisk is entirely untested (AIS_Why__c).

deal_momentum.text: The only recent movement is rep-side: stage churned Initial Interest to Qualified on 01 Jul 2026 and seven Call-Attempted health tasks were logged, none producing a buyer reply (SF field history + tasks).
The close date has slipped twice to 2027-02-26 with no buyer-confirmed timeline, and the May 2026 Gartner contact produced no follow-up (SF field history + Next_Step__c).

customer_commitment.text: Sasol has made no commitment: no next meeting requested, no RFI content shared, and no response to the workshop offer or the seven July outreach attempts (Next_Step__c + SF tasks 01 Jul 2026).
All five mapped contacts are operational Influencers with no advocacy shown and no budget owner among them (SF OpportunityContactRole).

deal_risk.text: Primary risk is no-decision / do-nothing inertia, not a rival: the buyer keeps managing procurement analytics manually and has gone quiet for 172 days (Next_Step__c + SF LastActivityDate).
No power access: the budget-owning Economic Buyer above VP Inbound Supply Chain is unidentified, so even a revived conversation lacks a path to sign-off (SF OpportunityContactRole).

commentary.win_position: Win 10 is carried by competitive strength +0.50 (weight 15); momentum 54 vs stage-expected 50. Offsetting it: preference strength -0.50 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 54 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 1 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.deal_risk: Risk 17 is driven by Verdict 'Off Track' — weak forward intent. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 0 is anchored on the stage win (10), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 54, and dragged by risk 17. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
